q: India is located in which hemisphere entirely?
sol: Geographically, the entire main land of India lies in the Northern Hemisphere.

q: Which imaginary line divides India into almost two equal parts?
sol: The Tropic of Cancer (23° 30'N) passes through the middle of the country.

q: The Himalayan mountains are classified as:
sol: The Himalayas are young fold mountains formed by the collision of tectonic plates.

q: Which of the following is the southernmost tip of the Indian mainland?
sol: While Indira Point is the southernmost point of the territory, Kanyakumari is the tip of the mainland.

q: The Northern Plains of India are formed by the alluvial deposits of which three major rivers?
sol: The interaction of the Indus, Ganga, and Brahmaputra created the fertile Northern Plains.

q: In which state is the 'Thar Desert' primarily located?
sol: The Great Indian Desert, or Thar Desert, lies mostly in the state of Rajasthan.

q: The Peninsular Plateau of India is roughly in which shape?
sol: The plateau is a triangular landmass that lies to the south of the river Narmada.

q: Which water body lies to the West of the Indian peninsula?
sol: The Arabian Sea borders India on its western coast.

q: The group of islands located in the Bay of Bengal is:
sol: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are situated in the Bay of Bengal.

q: Western Ghats are also known as:
sol: Sahyadri is the local name for the Western Ghats in Maharashtra and Karnataka.

q: Which is the highest peak in the world, located in the Himalayas?
sol: Mount Everest, at 8848m, is the world's highest peak located in the Himalayan range.

q: The Lakshadweep islands are known as ________ islands.
sol: Lakshadweep islands are formed from coral deposits called atolls.

q: Which river is often called the 'Dakshin Ganga'?
sol: Godavari is the largest peninsular river and is referred to as Dakshin Ganga due to its size.

q: The coastal plain along the Arabian Sea is called the:
sol: The strip of land between the Western Ghats and the Arabian Sea is the Western Coastal Plain.

q: Which mountains act as a natural barrier between India and Central Asia?
sol: The Himalayas prevent cold winds from Central Asia from entering India.

q: Standard Meridian of India passes through which city?
sol: The 82°30'E longitude passing through Mirzapur (UP) is the Standard Meridian.

q: Which of the following rivers flows into the Arabian Sea?
sol: Narmada and Tapti are major west-flowing rivers that fall into the Arabian Sea.

q: The oldest mountain range in India is the:
sol: The Aravali range is one of the oldest fold mountain systems in the world.

q: The delta formed by the Ganga and Brahmaputra is known as:
sol: The Sunderban Delta is the world's largest and fastest-growing delta.

q: Which island country is a southern neighbor of India across the Palk Strait?
sol: Sri Lanka is separated from India by the Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar.

q: The land of 'Five Rivers' refers to which state?
sol: Punjab gets its name from 'Punj' (five) and 'ab' (water/rivers).

q: Which part of India receives the highest rainfall in the world?
sol: Mawsynram in Meghalaya holds the record for the highest average rainfall.

q: Macha-like vegetation in the Himalayas at very high altitudes is called:
sol: Alpine vegetation is found at altitudes above 3600 meters.

q: The Eastern Coastal Plain is known as ________ in its southern part.
sol: The southern stretch of the Eastern Coastal Plain is the Coromandel Coast.

q: Which physical feature of India is a storehouse of minerals?
sol: The plateau region is rich in metallic and non-metallic minerals like coal and iron ore.

q: The cold desert in India is found in:
sol: Ladakh is a high-altitude cold desert located in the Himalayas.

q: The longitudinal valleys lying between Lesser Himalaya and Shiwaliks are known as:
sol: Dehra Dun, Kotli Dun, and Patli Dun are famous examples.

q: Which river originates from the Mansarovar Lake region?
sol: The Indus and Brahmaputra both originate near the Mansarovar Lake in Tibet.

q: The 'Seven Sister' states are located in which part of India?
sol: The contiguous states of North-East India are collectively called the Seven Sisters.

q: The Deccan Plateau slopes towards the:
sol: The plateau slopes gently towards the East, which is why most rivers flow into the Bay of Bengal.

q: The 'Palk Strait' lies between India and which country?
sol: The Palk Strait is a narrow strip of water separating India from Sri Lanka.

q: Which state has the longest coastline in India?
sol: Gujarat has the longest coastline due to its many indentations and gulfs.

q: The world's largest white salt desert is located in which Indian district?
sol: The Rann of Kutch in Gujarat is a famous salt marsh/desert.

q: Which range is located between the Narmada and Tapti rivers?
sol: The Satpura range lies in the Deccan plateau between these two rift-valley rivers.

q: The word 'Himalaya' means:
sol: In Sanskrit, 'Him' means snow and 'Alaya' means abode.

q: Which of these is a major tributary of the river Ganga?
sol: The Yamuna is the longest tributary of the Ganga, joining it at Prayagraj.

q: The highest peak of the Peninsular Plateau is:
sol: Anamudi (2695m) in Kerala is the highest peak of the Western Ghats and Peninsular India.

q: The Brahmaputra river is known as ________ in Tibet.
sol: In Tibet, the Brahmaputra is called the Tsangpo, meaning 'The Purifier'.

q: The Shiwalik range is the ________ range of the Himalayas.
sol: The Shiwaliks are the southernmost and outermost range of the Himalayas.

q: India's only active volcano is found on which island?
sol: Barren Island in the Andaman and Nicobar group is India's only active volcano.

q: Lakshadweep islands are situated in which sea?
sol: Lakshadweep is located in the Arabian Sea, off the coast of Kerala.

q: The Northern Plains are densely populated because of:
sol: Fertile alluvium and perennial rivers make agriculture highly productive there.

q: Which of the following is a east-flowing river?
sol: Mahanadi flows eastward and drains into the Bay of Bengal.

q: The meeting point of the Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats is:
sol: The two ranges converge at the Nilgiri Hills in Southern India.

q: What is the total area of the Indian landmass (approx)?
sol: India covers an area of about 3.28 million square kilometers.

q: India is the ________ largest country in the world by area.
sol: India is the 7th largest country in terms of geographical area.

q: The 'Himadri' is also known as:
sol: The Great or Inner Himalayas are called Himadri.

q: The landmass surrounded by water on three sides is known as a:
sol: India is called a peninsula because it is surrounded by water on three sides.

q: Western Coastal Plain is ________ compared to the Eastern Coastal Plain.
sol: The Western coastal strip is relatively narrow compared to the broad Eastern plains.

q: The state of Sikkim is bordered by which countries?
sol: Sikkim shares international borders with Nepal, Bhutan, and China (Tibet).

q: The coastal strip in Maharashtra and Goa is called:
sol: The northern part of the western coast is called the Konkan coast.

q: The Sundarbans are famous for which type of forest?
sol: Sundarbans are the largest mangrove forests in the world.

q: Which range forms the northern boundary of the Peninsular Plateau?
sol: The Vindhyan range roughly separates North India from the Plateau region.

q: The river Luni flows in which region?
sol: Luni is the only significant river in the Thar Desert region.

q: The distance between the North and South of India (Kashmir to Kanyakumari) is about:
sol: The North-South extent is approximately 3,214 km.

q: The distance between the East and West of India (Arunachal to Kuchchh) is about:
sol: The East-West extent is approximately 2,933 km.

q: Which state is known as the 'Fruit Bowl' of India?
sol: Himachal Pradesh is widely known as the fruit bowl due to its apple and stone fruit production.

q: The Purvanchal mountains are located in which part of India?
sol: The hills on the eastern boundary of India are called Purvanchal.

q: The river Krishna rises near which place?
sol: The Krishna river originates in the Western Ghats near Mahabaleshwar.

q: Which is the smallest state of India in terms of area?
sol: Goa is the smallest state by geographical area.

q: What kind of climate does India generally have?
sol: India’s climate is described as a tropical monsoon type.

q: The soil found in the Northern Plains is:
sol: Alluvial soil is deposited by the river systems in the plains.

q: The black soil region of the Peninsular Plateau is known as:
sol: The Deccan Trap is famous for its black cotton soil of volcanic origin.

q: The river Narmada originates in which hills?
sol: Narmada rises in the Amarkantak hills in Madhya Pradesh.

q: Which of the following is NOT a part of the Himalayan ranges?
sol: Sahyadri is part of the Western Ghats, not the Himalayas.

q: The 'Coromandel Coast' receives heavy rainfall during which season?
sol: Northeast/Retreating monsoons bring rain to the Tamil Nadu coast in winter.

q: The standard time of India is ahead of Greenwich Mean Time by:
sol: IST is GMT + 5:30.

q: The Chota Nagpur Plateau is famous for its ________ deposits.
sol: It is extremely rich in coal, iron, and mica minerals.

q: Which mountain range separates the North Indian plains from the Deccan Plateau?
sol: The Vindhyas act as a divide between the northern and southern parts of the country.

q: The Kaziranga National Park is located in which state?
sol: Assam is home to Kaziranga, famous for one-horned rhinoceroses.

q: Which is the largest salt water lake in India?
sol: Chilika Lake in Odisha is the largest brackish water lagoon in India.

q: The Western Ghats are ________ compared to the Eastern Ghats.
sol: Western Ghats are higher and can be crossed through passes only.

q: The highest peak of the Aravali range is:
sol: Guru Shikhar (1722m) in Rajasthan is the highest point of the Aravalis.

q: Which physical division of India was formed by the Tethys Sea?
sol: The Himalayas rose out of the Tethys Sea due to compression of sediments.

q: The river Brahmaputra enters India through which state?
sol: It takes a 'U-turn' at Namcha Barwa and enters through Arunachal Pradesh.

q: Which canal shortened the distance between India and Europe?
sol: Opening of the Suez Canal in 1869 reduced the distance by 7,000 km.

q: The river Kaveri flows into which water body?
sol: Kaveri is an east-flowing river that drains into the Bay of Bengal.

q: The Lakshadweep islands comprise of how many islands (approx)?
sol: The archipelago consists of 36 islands.

q: The narrow gap in a mountain range providing a route is called a:
sol: Passes like Nathu La or Shipki La are routes across the mountains.

q: The Malabar Coast is famous for its:
sol: Kerala's coast is renowned for its beautiful lagoons and backwaters.

q: India shares its longest land boundary with which country?
sol: India shares a 4,096 km long border with Bangladesh.

q: The northern part of the Eastern Coastal Plain is known as:
sol: The northern stretch is the Northern Circars, while the southern is Coromandel.

q: Which state does NOT share a border with Pakistan?
sol: Haryana is a landlocked state and does not touch the international border.

q: The 'Deccan Trap' region is ideal for growing which crop?
sol: The black soil found here is perfect for cotton cultivation.

q: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
sol: The Kosi river frequently changes its course and causes devastating floods.

q: The Gulf of Mannar is located off the coast of:
sol: It lies between the southeastern tip of India and Sri Lanka.

q: Which state in India has the first sunrise?
sol: As the easternmost state, Arunachal Pradesh sees the sun first.

q: The 'Barkhans' are found in which physical region of India?
sol: Barkhans are crescent-shaped sand dunes common in the desert.

q: The total length of India's coastline, including islands, is:
sol: The mainland coast is 6100 km, while the total with islands is 7516.6 km.

q: Which mountain range is located in the southern part of the Peninsular Plateau?
sol: The Nilgiris are located in the southern part of the plateau.

q: The capital of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands is:
sol: Port Blair is the administrative center of the union territory.

q: The 'Himachal' range lies between:
sol: The Lesser Himalayas or Himachal lie between the Great Himalayas and Shiwaliks.

q: Which river does NOT flow into the Bay of Bengal?
sol: Tapti flows westward into the Arabian Sea.

q: India's location between East and West Asia gives it an advantage in:
sol: The central location in the Indian Ocean is strategic for sea routes.

q: The 'Indira Point' got submerged under water during which event?
sol: The southernmost point was submerged during the 2004 Tsunami.

q: Which state has the largest area under the Northern Plains?
sol: Uttar Pradesh has the most extensive portion of the fertile Ganga plain.

q: The Western Ghats have a ________ average height than the Eastern Ghats.
sol: Western Ghats average 900-1600m, while Eastern Ghats average around 600m.

q: The 'Doab' is the land between:
sol: Doab (Do = two, ab = water) is fertile land between two rivers.

q: Which of the following is a Trans-Himalayan river?
sol: The Indus originates beyond the main Himalayan range.

q: The physical diversity of India is a result of:
o: Geological processes over millions of years
sol: Diversity is caused by tectonic movements, weathering, and erosion over long periods.
